(2021) is cited as demonstrating that standard PINN training 'converges robustly across multi-scale and convection-dominated regimes'—the stated justification for omitting curriculum training—but that paper's central finding was precisely the opposite: systematic failure modes in convection-dominated problems. If this attribution is reversed, the methodological warrant for a key design choice collapses."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking for an assessment of the scientific argument and validity of the conclusions, or for feedback on the writing and presentation quality?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact has a stated thesis (human eval as L2 complement) and a contribution claim (near-chance discrimination as evidence of solver parity); any general 'what do you think' reading requires assessing whether these are coherently framed and internally consistent.
rationale.R1.2: The central inferential move—53.1% near-chance discrimination implies physical faithfulness, which licenses full solver substitution in engineering pipelines—contains a critical logical gap; this is load-bearing under any scientific-review interpretation of the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: A single perceptual benchmark (one flow regime, one Re, velocity-magnitude only) producing a marginally above-chance result is used to anchor sweeping claims about adoption across 'incompressible-flow problems in the moderate-Reynolds regime'; evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing regardless of delegation interpretation.
rationale.R1.7: Krishnapriyan et al. (2021) is cited as demonstrating that standard PINN training 'converges robustly across multi-scale and convection-dominated regimes'—the stated justification for omitting curriculum training—but that paper's central finding was precisely the opposite: systematic failure modes in convection-dominated problems. If this attribution is reversed, the methodological warrant for a key design choice collapses.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for an assessment of the scientific argument and validity of the conclusions, or for feedback on the writing and presentation quality?
